# rustybox

> A free-range, non-GMO fork of BusyBox written entirely in Rust. One
> fully-static binary, dual-arch (x86_64 + aarch64), memory-safe applets on
> uutils/ripgrep/walkdir, and `timeout` built in for agentic harnesses.
> GPL-2.0 (BusyBox lineage), MIT where separable.

## What it is

- One fully-static musl binary — drop into a container `FROM scratch`, a
  rescue image, or a sandbox. No libc, no dependencies.
- Common tools route to battle-tested Rust: uutils coreutils, ripgrep's
  engine for grep, walkdir for find — same CLI, memory-safe internals.
- Built for coding agents: `timeout`, `nohup`, `dd`, `shuf` and friends
  baked in, the userland an autonomous harness needs to bound and script work.
- Resurrected from a six-year-dead c2rust transpile.

## Install

```
curl -LO https://github.com/peterlodri-sec/rustybox/releases/latest/download/rustybox-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
chmod +x rustybox-* && ./rustybox-* echo hello
```
